channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Help Shape Canada’s Health Research Future: Apply to Join a CIHR Institute Advisory Board
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The Canadian Institutes of Health Research (CIHR) is inviting applications for the 2026 Institute Advisory Board (IAB) Membership Cycle, offering professionals, researchers, and community leaders an opportunity to influence the future of health research in Canada. These advisory boards play a critical role in guiding CIHR’s Institutes by providing expert advice, strategic perspectives, and community […]

The post Help Shape Canada’s Health Research Future: Apply to Join a CIHR Institute Advisory Board appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Canadian Institutes of Health Research (CIHR) is inviting applications for the 2026 Institute Advisory Board (IAB) Membership Cycle, offering professionals, researchers, and community leaders an opportunity to influence the future of health research in Canada.

These advisory boards play a critical role in guiding CIHR’s Institutes by providing expert advice, strategic perspectives, and community insight to strengthen Canada’s health research ecosystem.

Serving as an IAB member allows individuals to contribute to national health research priorities, collaborate with experts across disciplines, and ensure research initiatives reflect the needs of diverse communities.

Application Deadline: April 25, 2026

What Are CIHR Institute Advisory Boards?

Each of CIHR’s 13 Institutes has its own Institute Advisory Board composed of 8–14 volunteer members from Canada and abroad. These members provide advice to the Institute’s Scientific Director on a wide range of activities, including:

  • Research priorities and strategic planning

  • Knowledge mobilization strategies

  • Community engagement initiatives

  • Cross-Institute collaborations

  • Health policy and innovation

The boards represent a broad spectrum of expertise across the health research ecosystem, including academia, government, healthcare institutions, industry, and community organizations.

The Role of an Institute Advisory Board Member

IAB members serve in an independent advisory capacity, providing insights and recommendations that strengthen the direction and impact of health research initiatives.

Key Responsibilities

Members contribute by:

  • Advising on Institute initiatives and strategic research priorities

  • Encouraging interdisciplinary health research collaborations

  • Strengthening partnerships with stakeholders and communities

  • Supporting knowledge translation and research impact

  • Considering ethical, policy, diversity, and inclusion factors in research initiatives

Members also help ensure research priorities address emerging health challenges and the needs of diverse Canadian populations.

Leadership and Governance

Each Institute Advisory Board operates with a leadership structure that includes either:

  • A Chair and Vice-Chair, or

  • Two Co-Chairs

These leaders guide discussions, develop meeting agendas with the Scientific Director, and ensure that advice represents the collective views of the board.

Appointments are made by CIHR’s Governing Council based on expertise, experience, and representation needs.

Membership Term and Time Commitment

Selected members are invited to serve for:

  • Initial term: Up to 3 years

  • Possible renewal: Up to an additional 3 years

  • Maximum service: 6 years

Board meetings occur at least twice per year, either virtually or in person.

While membership is voluntary, CIHR reimburses travel and meeting-related expenses at standard Government of Canada rates.

Areas of Expertise Sought for 2026

For the 2026 cycle, CIHR Institutes are seeking applicants with expertise in areas such as:

  • Artificial intelligence and data science in health research

  • Indigenous health research and systems

  • Health policy development and implementation

  • Clinical research and trials

  • Precision medicine and oncology

  • Mental health and substance use policy

  • Nutrition and metabolic diseases

  • Population health and public health policy

  • Digital health and learning health systems

  • Gerontechnology and aging research

Many Institutes are also seeking representation from individuals with lived experience, government professionals, patient partners, and international experts.

Why Join a CIHR Institute Advisory Board?

Serving on an IAB offers a unique opportunity to influence national health research priorities while working alongside leaders from multiple sectors.

Impact Canada’s Health Research Agenda

Your insights can help shape policies and research strategies that improve health outcomes across the country.

Expand Your Professional Network

Collaborate with researchers, policymakers, clinicians, and community leaders from Canada and around the world.

Represent Diverse Voices

CIHR is committed to equity, diversity, and inclusion. Advisory board members help ensure health research reflects the needs of all Canadians.

Commitment to Equity, Diversity and Inclusion

CIHR actively encourages applications from individuals who bring diverse perspectives to the advisory boards.

This includes candidates who identify as Indigenous, Francophone, gender diverse, living with disabilities, or members of racialized and marginalized communities.

Representation across regions, sectors, and lived experiences is considered an essential part of building inclusive health research leadership.

How the Selection Process Works

Applications are reviewed during the annual membership cycle. Scientific Directors evaluate candidates based on:

  • Expertise and professional experience

  • Gaps in board representation

  • Diversity and regional representation

  • Current vacancies on each Institute’s board

Selected candidates help fill expertise or representation needs within specific Institutes.

Applications remain active in the IAB candidate pool for three years, allowing applicants to be considered for future openings.

Apply for the 2026 CIHR IAB Membership Cycle

Professionals interested in shaping the future of health research in Canada are encouraged to submit an application before the deadline.

APPLY HERE. 

Application Deadline: April 25, 2026

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more job opportunities, click here. 

Learn more and apply through the official CIHR Institute Advisory Boards page.

The post Help Shape Canada’s Health Research Future: Apply to Join a CIHR Institute Advisory Board appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Civic Science Fellow 2026 at the Institute for Advanced Study (IAS) – $86,667 Salary for an 18-Month Fellowship in Princeton, USA
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The Institute for Advanced Study (IAS) is inviting applications for the 2026 Civic Science Fellow position under The Public’s Science initiative, a groundbreaking effort designed to reshape the relationship between science and society. This 18-month fellowship offers an annual salary of $86,667 and provides a unique opportunity to work at one of the world’s most […]

The post Civic Science Fellow 2026 at the Institute for Advanced Study (IAS) – $86,667 Salary for an 18-Month Fellowship in Princeton, USA appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Institute for Advanced Study (IAS) is inviting applications for the 2026 Civic Science Fellow position under The Public’s Science initiative, a groundbreaking effort designed to reshape the relationship between science and society. This 18-month fellowship offers an annual salary of $86,667 and provides a unique opportunity to work at one of the world’s most prestigious research institutions located in Princeton, New Jersey, USA.

The Civic Science Fellow will join the Science, Technology, and Social Values Lab within the IAS School of Social Science, led by renowned scholar Professor Alondra Nelson. The initiative is a collaboration with the Science & Justice Research Center at the University of California, Santa Cruz, aiming to build a more inclusive and participatory model of research governance in the United States.

Through this role, the selected fellow will contribute to developing new frameworks for public engagement in science and policy, helping to advance a modern social contract for research that better serves diverse communities.

About the Civic Science Fellowship

The 2026 Civic Science Fellow will play a key role in advancing The Public’s Science initiative by supporting research, communication, and community engagement efforts that promote inclusive participation in science policy.

The full-time fellowship lasts 18 months and offers a collaborative environment where fellows work alongside leading scholars, researchers, and policy practitioners focused on science governance and public engagement.

Fellowship Responsibilities

The fellow will spend approximately 75% of their time supporting The Public’s Science initiative, while the remaining 25% focuses on professional development.

Key responsibilities include:

  • Designing and implementing a digital engagement platform to facilitate public input and dialogue around research policy.

  • Producing and curating accessible content, including essays, research summaries, and multimedia materials.

  • Supporting the editorial development of scholarly publications, including contributions to a special issue of The ANNALS of the American Academy of Political and Social Science.

  • Conducting research on participatory governance models, international science policy frameworks, and public engagement practices.

  • Coordinating peer-review processes and working with contributors to ensure clarity and accessibility.

  • Building partnerships across an emerging community of practice focused on public science.

  • Assisting in the preparation of policy documents, reports, and research manuscripts.

  • Representing the initiative at conferences, seminars, and professional meetings.

Professional Development Opportunities

In addition to project responsibilities, the fellowship offers extensive professional growth opportunities. Fellows will:

  • Develop expertise in science communication and public scholarship.

  • Gain hands-on experience translating academic research for policymakers, journalists, and civil society organizations.

  • Build professional networks across academic, policy, media, and civic sectors.

  • Participate in seminars, workshops, and interdisciplinary discussions at IAS.

  • Contribute to shaping a policy framework for the future of American research governance.

Salary and Benefits

The Civic Science Fellowship offers competitive compensation and a comprehensive benefits package.

Compensation and benefits include:

  • Annual salary of $86,667

  • Total compensation of approximately $131,000 for the full 18-month appointment

  • Medical, dental, and vision insurance

  • Life insurance coverage

  • Short- and long-term disability benefits

  • Flexible spending accounts

  • Paid holidays and sick leave

  • Retirement plan options

  • Tuition assistance

  • Employee assistance program

Eligibility and Qualifications

Applicants must demonstrate strong writing, research, and communication skills along with experience in digital communications and community engagement.

Minimum qualifications include:

  • M.A. or M.S. in science writing, multimedia journalism, or science and health journalism
    OR

  • Ph.D. in social sciences, sciences, or humanities

  • Experience in digital communications or online platform management

  • Strong editorial and analytical abilities

  • Ability to translate complex academic ideas for broad audiences

  • Demonstrated interest in the social, cultural, and political implications of science and technology

Preferred qualifications include:

  • Experience in science and technology studies, science policy, or science communication

  • A strong record of research or publications

  • Experience managing digital platforms or public engagement initiatives

  • Program or project management experience

  • Strategic and forward-thinking approach to collaborative projects

About the Institute for Advanced Study

Founded in 1930, the Institute for Advanced Study is one of the world’s leading independent research centers dedicated to the pursuit of fundamental knowledge. IAS has a long tradition of supporting academic freedom and innovative research.

The institute has hosted an extraordinary community of scholars, including:

  • 37 Nobel Laureates

  • 46 Fields Medalists

  • 24 Abel Prize Laureates

  • Numerous MacArthur Fellows and Wolf Prize recipients

IAS continues to serve as a global hub for interdisciplinary scholarship and transformative ideas.

Application Process

Interested applicants should submit:

  • A resume

  • A cover letter

Applications will be reviewed for the 2026 Civic Science Fellow position.

Deadline

No specific deadline has been publicly announced. Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible.

The post Civic Science Fellow 2026 at the Institute for Advanced Study (IAS) – $86,667 Salary for an 18-Month Fellowship in Princeton, USA appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: Higher Ed Faculty AI Fellowship 2026 for Educators Using AI
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Higher Ed Faculty AI Fellowship 2026 is a prestigious global program designed to empower academic professionals to lead the transformation of higher education through artificial intelligence. As institutions worldwide navigate the rapid integration of AI into teaching, research, and administration, this fellowship positions faculty members not just as adopters of technology, but as strategic […]

The post Apply Now: Higher Ed Faculty AI Fellowship 2026 for Educators Using AI appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Higher Ed Faculty AI Fellowship 2026 is a prestigious global program designed to empower academic professionals to lead the transformation of higher education through artificial intelligence. As institutions worldwide navigate the rapid integration of AI into teaching, research, and administration, this fellowship positions faculty members not just as adopters of technology, but as strategic leaders shaping the future of education.

This comprehensive guide explores every aspect of the fellowship—from its purpose and benefits to the step-by-step application process—helping you understand how to become part of this influential global community.

Overview: Shaping the Future of AI in Higher Education

The Higher Ed Faculty AI Fellowship is built for forward-thinking educators who are already experimenting with or implementing AI in their academic work. Rather than focusing solely on instructional techniques, the program emphasizes institutional transformation, policy development, and innovation at scale.

Participants join a global network of academic pioneers committed to:

  • Advancing AI-driven research and discovery

  • Enhancing student learning experiences

  • Developing responsible AI governance frameworks

  • Leading systemic change across higher education institutions

The fellowship is especially suited for faculty members who want to transition from individual innovation to institution-wide and global impact.

Fellowship Objectives and Vision

The core goal of the program is to cultivate a new generation of academic leaders who can:

  • Strategically integrate AI into higher education systems

  • Address complex challenges such as academic integrity, assessment redesign, and ethical AI use

  • Influence institutional and global conversations around AI in education

Fellows are expected to act as change agents, bridging the gap between emerging technologies and real-world academic applications.

Key Benefits of the Higher Ed Faculty AI Fellowship

1. Strategic Leadership Opportunities

Fellows gain a platform to lead conversations and initiatives around AI adoption within their institutions. This includes tackling high-level challenges such as:

  • Redesigning assessment models in the age of AI

  • Establishing governance and ethical guidelines

  • Integrating AI into institutional strategy

2. Global Recognition and Ambassador Role

Participants are positioned as AI Ambassadors, representing their institutions while guiding peers through the evolving AI landscape. This recognition enhances both academic credibility and professional visibility.

3. Access to a Global Network

The fellowship connects participants with a curated community of educators, researchers, and innovators from around the world. This network enables:

  • Cross-institutional collaboration

  • Knowledge sharing on AI applications

  • Joint research and innovation opportunities

4. Professional Growth and Skill Development

Fellows develop advanced competencies in:

  • AI tools and applications for education

  • Research innovation using AI

  • Strategic decision-making and leadership

These skills help participants remain competitive and influential in a rapidly evolving academic environment.

5. Institutional Impact

Beyond individual growth, fellows bring value back to their institutions by:

  • Leading AI-driven innovation projects

  • Enhancing teaching and learning systems

  • Ensuring their campuses remain at the forefront of technological advancement

How Fellows Make a Difference

Leading Institutional Transformation

Fellows are empowered to address critical challenges within their universities or colleges. This could involve:

  • Developing AI policies and frameworks

  • Transforming curriculum design

  • Improving student outcomes through AI integration

Amplifying Professional Impact

Through collaboration and visibility, fellows expand their influence beyond their institutions, contributing to global discussions on AI in higher education.

Driving Innovation Across Higher Education

The fellowship encourages participants to think beyond their immediate environment and contribute to system-wide advancements in education.

2026 Fellowship Program Details

The 2026 cohort marks the inaugural intake of the Higher Ed Faculty AI Fellowship, making it a unique opportunity to be among the first group of global pioneers in this space.

Fellowship Timeline

  • Applications are currently open for the 2026 cohort

  • The program is structured across multiple phases throughout the year (Q1 to Q3)

  • Selected fellows will participate in a series of collaborative, research, and leadership activities during this period

Applicants are encouraged to prepare early to ensure they meet all requirements and deadlines.

Step-by-Step Application Process

Admission into the fellowship is highly competitive and curated, targeting faculty who have already demonstrated meaningful engagement with AI.

Step 1: Initiate Your Candidacy

There are two pathways to apply:

  • Nomination Route: Receive a formal invitation through nomination by a Google leader

  • Direct Application: Submit your application independently through the official platform

This dual-entry system ensures both recognition of established leaders and openness to emerging innovators.

Step 2: Demonstrate Your Expertise

Applicants must submit a Portfolio of Innovation, which may include:

  • AI-related academic projects

  • Research publications

  • Redesigned courses incorporating AI

  • Case studies or practical implementations

This portfolio serves as evidence of your ability to apply AI meaningfully in academic settings.

Step 3: Propose an Impact Challenge

A critical component of the application is identifying a real-world challenge you intend to address during the fellowship.

This challenge should be relevant to:

  • Assessment and evaluation methods

  • Institutional AI policy or governance

  • Student learning outcomes

The goal is to ensure that every fellow contributes to solving a tangible and impactful problem.

Step 4: Submit a Letter of Recommendation

Applicants must include a formal endorsement from a:

  • Dean

  • Program Chair

The letter should:

  • Support your participation in the fellowship

  • Highlight your leadership potential

  • Confirm institutional backing for your proposed work

Who Should Apply?

The fellowship is ideal for:

  • University and college faculty members

  • Academic researchers integrating AI into their work

  • Educators leading curriculum innovation

  • Institutional leaders interested in AI policy and strategy

Applicants should already have hands-on experience with AI and a clear vision for how it can transform higher education.

Why This Fellowship Matters

Artificial intelligence is reshaping every aspect of higher education—from how students learn to how institutions operate. However, the success of this transformation depends on informed, ethical, and strategic leadership.

The Higher Ed Faculty AI Fellowship addresses this need by:

  • Empowering educators to lead AI adoption responsibly

  • Building a global community of experts

  • Driving innovation that benefits both institutions and learners

By participating, fellows play a direct role in shaping the future of education on a global scale.

In addition to this fellowship, there are numerous programs and communities available for educators at different stages of their careers. Engaging with such initiatives can further expand your impact, network, and expertise in AI and education.

The Higher Ed Faculty AI Fellowship 2026 is more than a professional development program—it is a leadership platform for transforming higher education through AI. With its focus on innovation, collaboration, and real-world impact, the fellowship offers a unique opportunity to influence the direction of academic institutions worldwide.

If you are an educator already exploring AI and looking to scale your impact, this fellowship provides the structure, network, and recognition needed to take your work to the next level.

Discover more similar opportunities Here

The post Apply Now: Higher Ed Faculty AI Fellowship 2026 for Educators Using AI appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNCCD Youth Caucus Call for Youth Consultants 2026 (Paid Opportunity for Young Researchers)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The UNCCD Youth Caucus is inviting applications for youth consultants to support a global assessment on youth engagement in the implementation of the United Nations Convention to Combat Desertification (UNCCD). This is a paid, short-term consultancy opportunity running from March to June 2026. It is designed for young professionals with experience in research, data collection, […]

The post UNCCD Youth Caucus Call for Youth Consultants 2026 (Paid Opportunity for Young Researchers) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The UNCCD Youth Caucus is inviting applications for youth consultants to support a global assessment on youth engagement in the implementation of the United Nations Convention to Combat Desertification (UNCCD).

This is a paid, short-term consultancy opportunity running from March to June 2026. It is designed for young professionals with experience in research, data collection, and youth engagement who are interested in contributing to global environmental policy processes.

About the Opportunity

The UNCCD Youth Caucus aims to:

  • Assess youth engagement in the implementation of the UNCCD

  • Evaluate the effectiveness of the Youth Engagement Strategy

  • Identify best practices and lessons learned

  • Provide recommendations to strengthen meaningful youth participation

  • Contribute to a report submitted to the Conference of the Parties (COP) under Decision 5/COP 16

Selected consultants will contribute to shaping evidence-based recommendations that inform global decision-making on youth participation in climate and environmental governance.

Key Responsibilities

Selected youth consultants will:

  • Support the development and circulation of surveys

  • Organise and facilitate consultations with young people

  • Conduct interviews and focus group discussions

  • Provide rapporteur support during meetings and consultations

  • Assist with data analysis, synthesis, and visualization

  • Contribute to drafting the final report for submission to COP

Benefits

  • Paid consultancy (details shared with shortlisted candidates)

  • Experience working within a UN-related global process

  • Opportunity to contribute to high-level policy and reporting

  • Strengthening of research, facilitation, and analytical skills

  • Engagement with an international network of youth leaders and experts

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants should have:

  • Experience in data collection, analysis, and visualization

  • Strong research and writing skills (with work samples)

  • Knowledge or interest in youth engagement within UN processes (UNCCD experience is an advantage)

  • A proactive mindset with the ability to contribute innovative ideas

  • Ability to work collaboratively in a diverse, global team

Age requirement:

  • Applicants must be below 35 years old

Timeline

  • Consultancy period: March to June 2026

  • Application: Open now

Why You Should Apply

This opportunity provides a platform to:

  • Influence global environmental and climate policy discussions

  • Support evidence-based youth engagement strategies

  • Build experience within international development and UN processes

  • Contribute to advancing meaningful youth participation globally

How to Apply

Interested applicants should complete the official application form provided by the UNCCD Youth Caucus.

Application link: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSex6WbmJ_xgSO0Z_XK7gpchoYn2F0fJTFcOFL3qWzfqWi2M5Q/viewform

Explore More Opportunities

For more scholarships, internships, fellowships, and grants:

https://opportunitiesforyouth.org

The post UNCCD Youth Caucus Call for Youth Consultants 2026 (Paid Opportunity for Young Researchers) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for the Fully Funded MOOD for Climate Action Program 2026 (Global Youth Opportunity)
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Meaningful Open Opportunities for Discovery (MOOD) for Climate Action is a transformative, fully funded program designed to empower young people to take meaningful action against climate change. This experiential learning journey equips participants with the knowledge, skills, and confidence needed to move from climate anxiety to climate action—while building impactful projects and joining a global […]

The post Apply for the Fully Funded MOOD for Climate Action Program 2026 (Global Youth Opportunity) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Meaningful Open Opportunities for Discovery (MOOD) for Climate Action is a transformative, fully funded program designed to empower young people to take meaningful action against climate change. This experiential learning journey equips participants with the knowledge, skills, and confidence needed to move from climate anxiety to climate action—while building impactful projects and joining a global network of changemakers.

From Climate Anxiety to Climate Action

Climate change is no longer a distant concern—it is a present and urgent global challenge. Yet, many educators and young people feel unprepared to address it effectively. Studies show that while most teachers recognize the importance of climate education, fewer than half feel equipped to teach it. At the same time, a significant majority of young people express fear about the future and concern over global inaction.

The MOOD for Climate Action program responds to this gap by going beyond theory. It focuses on empowering learners not only to understand climate challenges but also to take meaningful, localized action while managing the emotional impact of the crisis.

Rather than viewing climate change solely as a crisis, this program reframes it as an opportunity—for self-discovery, collaboration, innovation, and leadership.

What the MOOD Journey Offers

This program is a highly scalable and immersive learning experience designed to build agency and real-world impact. Participants will:

  • Discover their personal purpose and how they can contribute to the world

  • Gain a systemic understanding of climate challenges and solutions

  • Take action on a local climate-related issue

  • Build essential lifelong skills, including:

    • Systems thinking

    • Leadership and collaboration

    • Social entrepreneurship

    • Critical thinking

    • Cross-cultural teamwork

    • Emotional intelligence and regulation

    • Project design and management

  • Join a global community of like-minded young changemakers

  • Enhance their CV with a project showcase and certification

Each completed module earns a certificate issued by a United Nations University (UNU) Hub, adding strong international recognition to participants’ profiles.

Who Can Apply?

The MOOD for Climate Action program is open to:

  • Young adults aged 15 to 35 years

  • Individuals available for at least 5 hours per week

  • Those passionate about climate action and personal development

  • Applicants willing to actively participate in collaborative projects

  • Individuals with a stable internet connection for virtual sessions

  • Participants with proficiency in English for global collaboration

Program Structure: 3 Certified Modules

Participants can choose to complete one, two, or all three modules depending on their availability and goals. Each module includes certification upon completion.

Key Highlights:

  • Each module = 1 certification from a UNU Hub

  • Flexible participation based on your schedule

  • Top-performing projects receive incubation support from the INCO incubator to ensure long-term sustainability

A Holistic Learning Experience

The MOOD program is built around three core dimensions:

1. Thematic (Knowledge)

Participants explore:

  • Causes and impacts of climate change

  • Climate justice and inequalities

  • Sustainable solutions and carbon reduction strategies

  • Adaptation approaches across different global regions

  • Systems thinking and long-term climate foresight

2. Socio-Emotional (Self)

This dimension focuses on personal development through:

  • Self-awareness exercises

  • Emotional regulation techniques

  • Group collaboration and communication skills

3. Action (Impact)

Participants gain hands-on experience by:

  • Identifying local climate challenges

  • Conducting fieldwork and interviews

  • Designing and refining solutions

  • Developing entrepreneurial and project implementation skills

Pedagogical Infrastructure & Learning Experience

The program combines flexibility with high-quality support and interactive learning tools.

Workload

  • 12 hours of live workshops and mentoring per module

  • Minimum 24 hours total commitment per module

  • Flexible schedule compatible with studies, jobs, or internships

Expert Support

Participants learn from a diverse team of experts, including:

  • Psychologists (for emotional and mental resilience)

  • Social entrepreneurs (for project development)

  • Climatologists (for scientific insights)

  • Dedicated mentors for project-based modules

Digital Learning Platform

  • Interactive, AI-enhanced learning environment

  • Real-time collaboration with global peers

  • Continuous community engagement and support

Certification & Career Benefits

Upon completing each module, participants receive a certification from a United Nations University Hub in France, enhancing their academic and professional profiles.

Additionally, participants:

  • Build a portfolio-ready climate project

  • Gain international exposure

  • Access opportunities for project incubation and scaling

Why You Should Apply

If you are passionate about climate action and want to move beyond awareness into real-world impact, this program provides the tools, network, and support you need.

“In the MOOD for Climate Action” is more than a course—it’s a launchpad for the next generation of climate leaders.

How to Apply

Interested candidates can:

  • Apply to the standard program

  • Apply for a fully funded cohort

  • Register Here, to begin their journey

For more information, applicants are encouraged to visit the official program page or contact the organizers directly.

Climate change demands urgent and collective action. Programs like MOOD for Climate Action are bridging the gap between knowledge and action by empowering young people with the skills, mindset, and opportunities needed to drive real change.

If you are ready to become a climate changemaker, this could be your moment.

Visit here for more details about the Learning institute.

Discover more similar opportunities by clicking Here.

The post Apply for the Fully Funded MOOD for Climate Action Program 2026 (Global Youth Opportunity) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Virginia Governor’s Fellows Program 2026: Paid Eight-Week Fellowship in the Office of the Governor for Future Public Service Leaders
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Virginia Governor’s Fellows Program 2026 offers an exceptional opportunity for students passionate about public service, policy development, and government leadership to gain first-hand experience working within the Office of the Governor of Virginia. Designed to cultivate the next generation of public leaders, this prestigious program provides selected fellows with an immersive eight-week professional experience […]

The post Virginia Governor’s Fellows Program 2026: Paid Eight-Week Fellowship in the Office of the Governor for Future Public Service Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Virginia Governor’s Fellows Program 2026 offers an exceptional opportunity for students passionate about public service, policy development, and government leadership to gain first-hand experience working within the Office of the Governor of Virginia.

Designed to cultivate the next generation of public leaders, this prestigious program provides selected fellows with an immersive eight-week professional experience inside the Governor’s Administration. Participants contribute to real policy initiatives, support daily government operations, and collaborate with experienced policymakers while building valuable professional skills.

The program provides an inside look at how state government functions and gives participants an opportunity to engage with the processes that shape policies impacting millions of citizens across the Commonwealth of Virginia.

Participants also receive a stipend for their two-month service, making this fellowship both a professional development opportunity and a paid experience in public service.

Overview of the Virginia Governor’s Fellows Program

The Governor’s Fellows Program places participants directly within the Governor’s Office and related administrative units, where they assist with key initiatives that support governance and public administration.

Fellows work closely with policy advisors, senior administrators, and other government professionals while contributing to projects that advance the priorities of the Commonwealth.

This program provides fellows with:

  • Hands-on experience in state government operations

  • Exposure to public policy development and implementation

  • Professional mentorship from government leaders

  • Opportunities to collaborate with fellow participants on impactful policy initiatives

The program is designed to equip participants with the knowledge, skills, and professional connections necessary for careers in public service, policy, law, governance, and civic leadership.

Fellowship Structure and Learning Components

The Virginia Governor’s Fellows Program combines practical work experience with structured professional development activities. Fellows not only contribute to daily operations but also complete several program components that deepen their understanding of government and policy.

1. Office Placement and Administrative Work

Each fellow is placed within an office in the Governor’s Administration based on their academic background, interests, and professional goals.

In their assigned offices, fellows may assist with:

  • Policy research and analysis

  • Administrative and operational support

  • Legislative and policy briefings

  • Public service initiatives

  • Special projects relevant to the administration’s priorities

This placement ensures that participants gain meaningful exposure to how state government functions on a day-to-day basis.

2. Professional Portfolio Development

During the fellowship, participants develop a professional portfolio that highlights their work and growth throughout the program.

The portfolio may include:

  • Research outputs

  • Policy analysis contributions

  • Reports or briefings completed during the program

  • Reflections on professional development and learning experiences

This portfolio serves as a valuable professional asset that fellows can use in future career and academic opportunities.

3. Policy Project

Another central component of the fellowship is the policy project.

Fellows work in small teams to research and develop solutions for policy issues affecting the Commonwealth of Virginia.

The policy project includes:

  1. Identifying a relevant policy challenge

  2. Conducting research and data analysis

  3. Developing policy recommendations

  4. Presenting findings to administration officials

At the conclusion of the fellowship, fellows present their projects in a formal policy briefing, demonstrating their analytical skills and ability to develop actionable solutions.

Key Benefits of the Program

The Virginia Governor’s Fellows Program offers numerous benefits that support participants’ academic, professional, and leadership development.

Professional Experience in Government

Participants gain valuable insights into the inner workings of state government, policy development, and administrative operations.

Paid Fellowship Experience

Each fellow receives a stipend for their two-month service to the Commonwealth of Virginia.

Policy Development Experience

Through the policy project and office assignments, fellows develop practical skills in:

  • Policy analysis

  • Government research

  • Strategic problem solving

  • Public sector communication

Networking and Mentorship

Fellows have opportunities to connect with:

  • Senior government officials

  • Policy advisors

  • Public service leaders

  • Fellow students interested in governance and public administration

These connections can help launch careers in government, policy, law, and public leadership.

Who Should Apply

The Governor’s Fellows Program welcomes applicants who demonstrate strong commitment to public service and leadership.

Eligible applicants include:

  • Rising college seniors

  • Graduating seniors

  • Graduate students

Applicants must also meet one of the following conditions:

  • Be enrolled at a Virginia college or university, or

  • Be a Virginia resident studying at an out-of-state institution

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Academic excellence

  • Leadership potential

  • Civic engagement and community service

  • Participation in extracurricular activities and initiatives

The program seeks candidates who are motivated, thoughtful, and eager to contribute to the work of the Commonwealth.

Program Timeline

Applicants should carefully review the timeline for the 2026 fellowship cycle.

Application Deadline

April 10, 2026

All application materials must be submitted by this date. Late or incomplete submissions will not be considered.

Interview Phase

April 21, 2026

Selected applicants will participate in virtual interviews with program officials.

Final Decision Notifications

April 30, 2026

All applicants will receive notification regarding their application status.

Fellowship Program Dates

June 8 – July 31, 2026

The fellowship is conducted full-time and in person during this period.

Participants must be available for the entire duration of the program.

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit a complete application package through the official online portal.

Required application materials include:

  1. Completed online application form

  2. Résumé outlining academic and professional experience

  3. Official or unofficial academic transcript

  4. Two letters of recommendation from academic or professional references

  5. Essay explaining commitment to public service and goals for participation

  6. Selection of up to three areas of interest for placement consideration

These materials allow the selection committee to evaluate candidates based on their qualifications, leadership potential, and commitment to public service.

Selection Process

Fellows are selected through a competitive merit-based review process.

The program follows equal opportunity principles and does not discriminate based on:

  • Race

  • Sex

  • Color

  • National origin

  • Religion

  • Sexual orientation

  • Gender identity

  • Age

  • Political affiliation

  • Disability

  • Veteran status

Applicants are evaluated based on academic performance, leadership potential, civic engagement, and commitment to public service.

Career Impact of the Governor’s Fellows Program

Many past fellows credit the program as a launchpad for successful careers in government and policy leadership.

Participants gain:

  • Professional experience in high-level policymaking environments

  • Mentorship from government leaders

  • Exposure to real public policy challenges

  • Networking opportunities that support long-term career development

Several alumni have gone on to serve in influential roles within state government, federal agencies, and policy organizations.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can learn more and submit their application through the Official Governor of Virginia website.

Applicants are encouraged to prepare their materials early and ensure all required documents are submitted before the deadline.

Click HERE for more information about the Virginia Governor’s Fellows Program 2026.

Discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Virginia Governor’s Fellows Program 2026: Paid Eight-Week Fellowship in the Office of the Governor for Future Public Service Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for the Key Habitats Program 2026 — Funding & Support for Conservation and Community Initiatives in the Caribbean
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
Conservationists, community leaders, NGOs, and environmental project teams in the Caribbean now have a valuable opportunity to apply for the Key Habitats Program 2026, a funding initiative designed to support projects that conserve critical ecosystems and promote sustainable, community‑driven solutions. Led by the Dutch Caribbean Nature Alliance (DCNA), the program provides financial support, technical guidance, […]

The post Apply for the Key Habitats Program 2026 — Funding & Support for Conservation and Community Initiatives in the Caribbean appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Conservationists, community leaders, NGOs, and environmental project teams in the Caribbean now have a valuable opportunity to apply for the Key Habitats Program 2026, a funding initiative designed to support projects that conserve critical ecosystems and promote sustainable, community‑driven solutions.

Led by the Dutch Caribbean Nature Alliance (DCNA), the program provides financial support, technical guidance, and collaborative networks to help protect and restore biodiversity, strengthen local conservation capacity, and integrate community needs with environmental stewardship. (dcnanature.org)

The Key Habitats Program targets initiatives that address priority conservation challenges, empowering local stakeholders to lead environmental action while contributing to long‑term ecosystem resilience across the Dutch Caribbean.

About DCNA and the Key Habitats Program

The Dutch Caribbean Nature Alliance is a regional partnership of nature conservation organizations across the Dutch Caribbean islands, including Aruba, Bonaire, Curaçao, Saba, Sint Eustatius, and Sint Maarten. DCNA works to protect biodiversity, strengthen institutional capacity, and ensure sustainable use of natural resources.

The Key Habitats Program serves as a strategic funding mechanism that facilitates impactful, community‑linked conservation projects. The initiative prioritizes efforts that are guided by scientific evidence and develop lasting environmental and social benefits.

By providing targeted funding and tailored support, the program helps emerging and established conservation efforts realize measurable outcomes.

Why This Opportunity Matters

The Caribbean region is home to some of the world’s most unique and vulnerable ecosystems, including coral reefs, mangroves, and tropical forests. These habitats are crucial not only for biodiversity but also for the livelihoods, food security, and cultural heritage of communities.

However, pressures such as climate change, habitat degradation, invasive species, and unsustainable use of resources threaten these ecosystems and the services they provide.

The Key Habitats Program supports locally led efforts that address these challenges by strengthening institutional capacity, elevating community voices in conservation, and providing tools and resources for impactful action.

For conservation practitioners, community leaders, and environmental advocates, this program offers a platform to implement meaningful change that resonates across ecological and social systems.

What the Program Offers

The Key Habitats Program provides a combination of financial resources, technical support, and collaborative engagement designed to elevate conservation projects from concept to action.

Financial Support

Selected applicants receive project funding that can be used to implement conservation activities, build capacity, or support community‑driven solutions. Funding helps cover essential project costs such as:

  • Fieldwork and data collection
  • Community education or outreach materials
  • Equipment and supplies
  • Training and facilitation activities

This financial support enables organizations and initiatives to execute conservation plans that might otherwise be limited by resource constraints.

Technical and Organizational Support

In addition to funding, the program offers technical guidance from conservation specialists and professionals. This may include support with:

  • Project planning and design
  • Monitoring and evaluation
  • Data analysis and reporting
  • Communications and stakeholder engagement

By connecting teams with technical expertise, DCNA ensures that projects are both scientifically sound and culturally relevant to local communities.

Networking and Collaboration

The Key Habitats Program encourages collaboration across the region. Participants gain access to:

  • Regional conservation networks
  • Peer learning opportunities
  • Knowledge exchange with practitioners and experts
  • Support from partner organizations in the Dutch Caribbean

These connections help strengthen long‑term capacity and foster partnerships that extend beyond the duration of individual projects.

Who Should Apply

The Key Habitats Program is open to a range of applicants working on conservation and sustainability initiatives within the Dutch Caribbean region. Eligible applicants include:

  • Nonprofit organizations engaged in conservation or community development
  • Community groups or cooperatives with environmental projects
  • Research teams working on habitat protection, restoration, or biodiversity studies
  • Youth‑led or community‑led initiatives with a focus on sustainability

Projects may vary in scope, but successful applications typically demonstrate a clear conservation objective, measurable outcomes, and strong community engagement.

Areas of Focus and Project Priorities

The Key Habitats Program prioritizes projects that contribute to the conservation of critical ecosystems and support local community resilience. Priority areas include:

  • Habitat protection and restoration
  • Biodiversity monitoring and research
  • Community engagement and environmental education
  • Sustainable livelihood integration with conservation goals

Projects that link environmental outcomes to community benefits — such as sustainable fishing practices, ecotourism, or food security solutions — are particularly encouraged.

By emphasizing both ecological and social dimensions, the program seeks to create holistic approaches that benefit both nature and people.

Application Process and Timeline

Interested applicants should review the Key Habitats Program guidelines and submit their proposals through the official DCNA application process.

Applications typically require:

  • A clear project description
  • Objectives and measurable outcomes
  • Budget and resource plan
  • Letters of support or partner endorsements

While DCNA publishes specific timelines and deadlines for each funding round, applicants are encouraged to prepare materials well in advance to ensure completeness and competitive quality.

Application Deadline: 13 April 2026

Applicants should consult the official DCNA Key Habitats Program page for more submission details.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Apply for the Key Habitats Program 2026 — Funding & Support for Conservation and Community Initiatives in the Caribbean appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Applications Open for Claude Community Ambassadors Program 2026
item.dc:creator: Ankita Baidya
item.description:
Deadline: Rolling Applications Anthropic has opened applications for the Claude Community Ambassadors Program, a global initiative designed to support individuals who want to build and lead local communities around Claude. The program invites community builders, technical users, and AI enthusiasts to host events, share knowledge, and contribute to shaping the future of AI tools. […]

The post Applications Open for Claude Community Ambassadors Program 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Deadline: Rolling Applications

Anthropic has opened applications for the Claude Community Ambassadors Program, a global initiative designed to support individuals who want to build and lead local communities around Claude. The program invites community builders, technical users, and AI enthusiasts to host events, share knowledge, and contribute to shaping the future of AI tools.

The initiative focuses on empowering individuals to create vibrant local ecosystems where builders, developers, and curious learners can come together to explore and experiment with Claude. By supporting grassroots community efforts, Anthropic aims to expand access to AI knowledge while fostering collaboration across regions.

About the Claude Community Ambassadors Program

The Claude Community Ambassadors Program is built around the idea that strong communities drive innovation. Ambassadors are expected to organise meetups, workshops, and hackathons in their cities, creating spaces where people can learn, build, and exchange ideas.

Participants are not required to be professional developers. While applicants should have meaningful experience using Claude tools such as Claude Code or Claude Cowork, the program places greater emphasis on community involvement and the ability to bring people together. This makes the opportunity accessible to a wide range of individuals, including educators, organisers, and tech enthusiasts.

What Ambassadors Receive

Selected ambassadors gain access to a range of resources to support their community-building efforts. These include:

  • Funding support for local events such as meetups and hackathons
  • Ready-to-use content and promotional assistance
  • Swag and materials to enhance community engagement
  • Monthly API credits to run demos and prototype ideas

In addition, ambassadors receive early access to pre-release features and can participate in Builders Council sessions, where they share feedback directly with Anthropic’s product teams.

Global and Flexible Participation

The program is open to applicants from anywhere in the world, reflecting Anthropic’s focus on geographic diversity. There is no requirement to be based in a specific location, and multiple ambassadors can operate within the same city. This flexibility allows communities to grow organically across regions and ensures that participation is not limited by geography.

Roles and Responsibilities

Ambassadors are expected to take an active role in growing their local communities. This includes hosting events, running live demonstrations, and encouraging collaboration among participants. They are also responsible for sharing insights from their communities with Anthropic, helping shape future product development.

Participants will join a private Slack network where they can connect with other ambassadors globally, exchange ideas, and collaborate on initiatives.

Compensation and Benefits

While the program does not provide direct financial compensation, it offers substantial support in other forms. Ambassadors receive event sponsorship, API credits, networking opportunities, promotional support, and recognition within the global AI community. These benefits are designed to help participants build impactful communities and expand their professional networks.

Application Process

Applications are open on a rolling basis, allowing interested individuals to apply at any time. Selected candidates will go through an onboarding process that includes signing an ambassador agreement, joining the private Slack workspace, and planning their first event.

There is no cost to join the program, and all resources are provided by Anthropic.

Why This Matters

As AI continues to shape industries and societies, community-led initiatives play an important role in ensuring that knowledge and access are widely distributed. The Claude Community Ambassadors Program supports individuals who want to take the lead in making AI tools more accessible, collaborative, and locally relevant.

For those passionate about community building and emerging technologies, the program offers a unique opportunity to contribute to a global network while creating impact at the local level.

Deadline: Rolling Applications

Application can be accessed from here.

To know more about such opportunities, click here.

The post Applications Open for Claude Community Ambassadors Program 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for STIAS Fellowships 2027 — Fully Funded Research Fellowships in South Africa
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
Are you a research scholar, scientist, or artist looking for dedicated time and resources to advance your work? The Stellenbosch Institute for Advanced Study (STIAS) is now accepting applications for it’s 2027 Fellowship Programme. This fully funded fellowship provides scholars and artists with the financial support, interdisciplinary community, and protected time needed to make meaningful […]

The post Apply for STIAS Fellowships 2027 — Fully Funded Research Fellowships in South Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you a research scholar, scientist, or artist looking for dedicated time and resources to advance your work? The Stellenbosch Institute for Advanced Study (STIAS) is now accepting applications for it’s 2027 Fellowship Programme. This fully funded fellowship provides scholars and artists with the financial support, interdisciplinary community, and protected time needed to make meaningful progress on ambitious research or creative projects.

Located in Stellenbosch, South Africa, STIAS brings together international and local fellows to foster collaboration, innovation, and intellectual exchange across a wide range of disciplines. Whether your work is in the sciences, humanities, arts, or social sciences, STIAS provides the environment to deepen your research, produce new work, and connect with peers and mentors who can help amplify your impact.

What the STIAS Fellowship Offers

Full Financial Support

Selected fellows receive comprehensive funding, including:

  • A fully equipped office
  • Weekday lunch & living stipend
  • Accommodation during the fellowship period
  • Travel to and from Stellenbosch, South Africa

This funding ensures fellows can dedicate themselves fully to their work without balancing additional employment or academic duties.

Protected Research or Creative Time

One of the greatest benefits of a STIAS fellowship is uninterrupted focus. Fellows are relieved of teaching, administrative, or other professional obligations, allowing them to:

  • Conduct in-depth research or experiments
  • Write academic publications, books, or reports
  • Develop creative or artistic projects
  • Experiment with interdisciplinary collaborations

This protected time enables scholars and artists to tackle ambitious projects that may be difficult to achieve in a typical academic or professional setting.

Interdisciplinary Engagement and Networking

STIAS emphasizes cross-disciplinary collaboration. Fellows interact with a diverse community of scholars, scientists, and artists, exchanging ideas and methodologies. This fosters innovation and can open doors to new perspectives and opportunities.

  • Attend seminars and workshops led by leading experts
  • Engage in informal peer discussions and idea exchanges
  • Build long-term professional networks locally, regionally, and internationally

Career and Academic Development

Beyond research and creative outputs, STIAS fellowships offer invaluable professional development:

  • Opportunities to collaborate on international research projects
  • Exposure to cutting-edge ideas across disciplines
  • Mentorship from established scholars and practitioners
  • Enhanced CV and professional profile for future grants, positions, or exhibitions

Why This Opportunity Matters

STIAS fellowships offer a rare chance to dedicate uninterrupted time to high-impact research or creative practice. For scholars, this can lead to publications, grant proposals, or collaborative projects that advance knowledge in their field. For artists, it can provide the time, resources, and community support to complete ambitious creative projects or develop new artistic directions.

The programme also contributes to South Africa’s academic and cultural landscape by bringing global expertise to Stellenbosch, fostering collaborations with local institutions, and enhancing cross-disciplinary dialogue.

Who Should Apply

The STIAS Fellowship Programme is open to both emerging and established scholars, scientists, and artists. Ideal applicants include:

  • Researchers with a strong record of publications or projects
  • Scientists conducting advanced studies in their field
  • Practicing artists or creative professionals with significant work in progress
  • Individuals who benefit from interdisciplinary engagement and mentorship

Applicants from any discipline are encouraged to apply, as long as the project will benefit from dedicated time, financial support, and STIAS’s collaborative environment.

Application Process and Deadline

Applicants should submit:

  • A detailed research or creative proposal
  • CV or portfolio
  • References or letters of support

Applications are accepted until the deadline listed on the official STIAS website. Fellows are selected based on the quality and feasibility of their projects, the potential impact of their work, and the alignment with STIAS’s interdisciplinary mission.

Application Deadline: 30 April 2026

Learn more and apply on the official STIAS website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Apply for STIAS Fellowships 2027 — Fully Funded Research Fellowships in South Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Applications Open for Rooted and Rising: Youth Leaders Grant 2026
item.dc:creator: Ankita Baidya
item.description:
Deadline: April 17, 2026 The Conrad Prebys Foundation has launched the Rooted and Rising: Youth Leaders Grant initiative, aimed at strengthening civic participation by investing in youth leadership programs across San Diego County. The initiative recognises that a healthy democracy depends on active engagement—yet many young people are still positioned as passive recipients rather than […]

The post Applications Open for Rooted and Rising: Youth Leaders Grant 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Deadline: April 17, 2026

The Conrad Prebys Foundation has launched the Rooted and Rising: Youth Leaders Grant initiative, aimed at strengthening civic participation by investing in youth leadership programs across San Diego County. The initiative recognises that a healthy democracy depends on active engagement—yet many young people are still positioned as passive recipients rather than active contributors to change.

This funding opportunity seeks to shift that narrative by supporting programs that equip young people with the skills, confidence, and platforms needed to engage meaningfully in civic life.

About the Youth Leaders Grant 2026

The Rooted and Rising initiative is designed to support programs that combine leadership development with real-world civic engagement. By focusing on middle and high school-aged youth, the program aims to nurture a generation capable of shaping more inclusive and equitable communities.

The foundation highlights that young people already bring creativity, resilience, and a strong desire to contribute. However, access to structured civic engagement opportunities remains uneven. Through this initiative, the foundation aims to bridge that gap and position youth as active partners in community problem-solving.

In recent years, there has been growing recognition that early exposure to civic processes—such as dialogue, community organising, and collaborative decision-making—can significantly shape long-term democratic participation. Programs that engage youth at this stage not only build individual confidence but also strengthen collective capacity within communities, ensuring that future leaders are better equipped to address complex social challenges.

Key Focus Areas

Funded programs must integrate skill-building with practical experience. This includes:

  • Leadership, collaboration, and problem-solving training
  • Experiential learning tied to real community challenges such as climate resilience, arts and culture, health, and democratic participation
  • Opportunities for youth to engage in storytelling, content creation, and visible leadership roles
  • Strong mentorship networks connecting youth with civic leaders and career pathways

A central requirement is that all programs culminate in an action project, where participants actively contribute to solving real community issues.

Funding Details

  • Grant Amount: Up to $150,000 per award
  • Total Funding Pool: $2.5 million
  • Number of Grants: Approximately 15–20
  • Grant Duration: July 2026 to December 2027 (18 months)
  • Funding Type: Programmatic

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be a 501(c)(3) nonprofit, fiscally sponsored organisation, or Tribal entity
  • Operate in and/or serve San Diego County
  • Focus on middle and/or high school-aged youth
  • Deliver programs centred on leadership development, civic engagement, and community problem-solving
  • Be an existing program that is expanding or strengthening its impact

Programs must also demonstrate ongoing engagement rather than one-time activities and show clear outcomes such as participant numbers, skill development, and types of civic engagement activities.

Program Expectations

Proposals should include measurable outcomes and clearly defined program goals. Applicants are encouraged to highlight:

  • Youth participation and leadership growth
  • Civic engagement activities such as volunteering, advisory councils, or peer-led initiatives
  • Use of youth-generated content like videos or social media storytelling
  • Inclusion of diverse perspectives and community-informed approaches

Preference will be given to programs that involve youth as co-designers or decision-makers and demonstrate strong community impact through structured frameworks or learning models.

Application Process and Timeline

  • Application Opens: March 9, 2026
  • Office Hours: March 17 – April 16, 2026
  • Application Deadline: April 17, 2026 (5:00 PM)
  • Notification of Results: Late May to early June 2026
  • Grant Period: July 2026 – December 2027

Applicants must register on the foundation’s grant portal before applying. Registration approval may take 2–3 days, so early preparation is recommended.

Why This Matters

By investing in youth leadership, the initiative aims to strengthen not just individual participants but the broader civic ecosystem of San Diego. The program envisions a future where young people see themselves as stakeholders in their communities—capable of driving meaningful, long-term change.

Organisations working at the intersection of youth development and civic engagement are encouraged to apply and contribute to building a more participatory and resilient society.

Deadline: April 17, 2026 

Application can be accessed from here.

To know more about such opportunities, click here.

The post Applications Open for Rooted and Rising: Youth Leaders Grant 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
